title: Andinia lunaris
taxonomy: Andinia lunaris is a species of orchid in the genus Andinia, which is part of the family Orchidaceae. The orchid family, Orchidaceae, is the largest plant family in the world, comprising over 30,000 species. Andinia lunaris is a type of terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on land rather than in water. The species Andinia lunaris is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a member of the subtribe Andiniinae, which is further classified within the tribe Maxillarieae.

intro: Andinia lunaris, also known as the Lunar Andinia, is a plant species within the family Ericaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, including wounds and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Andinia lunaris include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Andinia lunaris include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal benefits.
